question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `timestamp=eq:0` parameter to `GET /api/v1/network/exchangerate` to force retrieval of the genesis-era exchange rate from Hashgraph history, causing downstream fee calculations by integrators to use a wildly incorrect HBAR/USD rate and leading to financial mispricing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user send two conflicting `TimestampParameter` values (e.g., `timestamp=gt:X&timestamp=lt:X`) to `getExchangeRate()` such that `Bound.of()` constructs an inverted range, causing `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` to return an unexpected historical exchange rate snapshot that misrepresents the true Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Size(max = 2)` constraint on `TimestampParameter[] timestamp` by sending exactly 2 timestamps with operators `gt:T1` and `gt:T2` (both lower-bound operators) to `getExchangeRate()`, causing `Bound` to silently overwrite the lower bound and select a different historical exchange rate than intended, misrepresenting Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:1` (near-zero nanosecond timestamp) to `getExchangeRate()` to force `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` to scan the earliest possible Hashgraph history records, returning a pre-network-launch or corrupted exchange rate that misrepresents the true historical state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:9999999999.999999999` (maximum nanosecond timestamp) to `getExchangeRate()` to cause `Bound.adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, forcing a full-table scan of the `file_data` table and causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate access to current Hashgraph exchange rate history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft `timestamp=eq:T` pointing to a known past Hashgraph epoch where fee schedules were significantly lower to `GET /api/v1/network/fees`, causing `fileService.getFeeSchedule(bound)` to return stale historical fee data that integrators use to underpay transaction fees, exploiting the Hashgraph fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user send `timestamp=gt:0&timestamp=lt:1` to `getFees()` to construct a near-zero `Bound` that causes `fileService.getFeeSchedule(bound)` and `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` to both query the absolute earliest Hashgraph history, returning genesis-era fee data that misrepresents the current network state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getFees()` uses the same `Bound` for both `fileService.getFeeSchedule(bound)` and `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` by crafting a timestamp that selects a fee schedule from one Hashgraph epoch but an exchange rate from a different epoch (due to different file update frequencies), causing a mismatched historical state that produces incorrect fee estimates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user send two `eq` operator timestamps (e.g., `timestamp=eq:T1&timestamp=eq:T2`) to `getFees()` where `Bound` resolves the last-seen `eq` as the effective bound, causing `feeScheduleMapper.map()` to receive a `Bound` that selects a different historical fee schedule than the one at `T1`, silently misrepresenting Hashgraph fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an invalid `order` parameter value to `getFees()` that bypasses Spring's `Sort.Direction` enum parsing and causes an unhandled exception that leaks internal Hashgraph history query details in the error response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST an arbitrarily large protobuf body to `POST /api/v1/network/fees` (no size limit enforced in `estimateFees()`) to exhaust server memory during `Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(body))`, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate fee estimation based on current Hashgraph fee schedule history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a deeply nested or recursively structured protobuf payload and POST it to `estimateFees()` to trigger stack overflow or excessive CPU consumption in `Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(body))`, causing a denial-of-service that disrupts access to Hashgraph fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf-encoded transaction with a crafted `signedTransactionBytes` field containing a valid outer wrapper but malformed `bodyBytes` to `estimateFees()`, causing `TransactionFeeContext` to parse a different transaction body than intended and return a fee estimate that misrepresents the true Hashgraph fee schedule for that transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a transaction with both `signedTransactionBytes` and `bodyBytes` populated to `estimateFees()`, where `TransactionFeeContext` preferentially parses `signedTransactionBytes` (line 140 check), allowing the attacker to supply a different transaction body than what `bodyBytes` contains and obtain a fee estimate for a transaction type not reflected in the outer wrapper, misrepresenting Hashgraph fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a transaction with `mode=STATE` and `highVolumeThrottle=10000` to `estimateFees()` to force `FeeEstimationService` to read live DB state (custom fees, token stores) and return a fee estimate that reflects a manipulated or stale Hashgraph state snapshot, misleading integrators about true current fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a zero-byte body to `estimateFees()` (bypassing `@NotNull` with an empty array) to cause `Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(new byte[0]))` to return a default empty `Transaction`, which then causes `TransactionFeeContext` to throw `IllegalArgumentException('Transaction must contain body bytes or signed transaction bytes')`, leaking internal implementation details about Hashgraph transaction parsing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf transaction with an unknown/unsupported `HederaFunctionality` type to `estimateFees()` to cause `feeEstimationService.estimateFees()` to throw `IllegalArgumentException('Unknown transaction type')`, and by repeatedly probing different transaction types, enumerate which transaction types are supported in the current Hashgraph fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a transaction with `highVolumeThrottle=0` vs `highVolumeThrottle=10000` and `mode=STATE` to `estimateFees()` to observe the difference in `highVolumeMultiplier` in the response, thereby inferring the current Hashgraph fee schedule's high-volume pricing configuration (a Hashgraph history artifact) without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `toResponse()` function's `highVolumeMultiplier` calculation (`feeResult.getHighVolumeMultiplier() / HighVolumePricingCalculator.HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE`) to cause an integer division by zero if `HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE` is zero, crashing the service and preventing access to Hashgraph fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf transaction with an extremely large `memo` field or many signature pairs to `estimateFees()` to cause `numTxnBytes()` (which calls `Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord(transaction)`) to return an inflated byte count, causing the fee estimate to reflect a transaction size that does not match the actual Hashgraph fee schedule parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call `GET /api/v1/network/stake` at high frequency to cause `networkService.getLatestNetworkStake()` to perform repeated DB queries against the `network_stake` table, exhausting DB connection pool resources and preventing legitimate reads of Hashgraph staking history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getNetworkStake()` always returns the latest stake data (no timestamp filter) to observe that the returned `NetworkStakeResponse` reflects a specific Hashgraph consensus period, and by comparing responses over time, infer when the Hashgraph network stake history was last updated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a `404 EntityNotFoundException` from `getNetworkStake()` when no stake data exists in the mirror node DB, and use this signal to infer that the Hashgraph staking history has not yet been ingested, leaking information about the mirror node's synchronization state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=eq:0` to `GET /api/v1/network/supply` to force `networkService.getSupply(bound)` to query `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()` at the genesis timestamp, returning a supply figure from the very beginning of Hashgraph history that misrepresents the current circulating supply?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `q=TOTALCOINS` to `getSupply()` to receive the hardcoded `NetworkSupply.TOTAL_SUPPLY = 5_000_000_000_000_000_000L` value regardless of the actual Hashgraph history, and use this to mislead downstream systems that treat this endpoint as authoritative for the true circulating supply?
